channel.title: blog on Arjun's Webpage
channel.description: Recent content in blog on Arjun's Webpage

item.title: Pentax DL-9000 : The Scamera
item.description: A collection of Pentax DL9000 listings I’ve found on my hunt for a SLR camera.

item.title: Ender 3
item.description: An Ender 3 is the rite of passage for all 3D Printing enthusiasts. Yes a Bambu lab may be refined, convenient and clean but to become the ultimate hobbyist and maker one must burn their hands cleaning the clogged extruder of an Ender 3. One must blow more money on repairs than the actual cost of the printer in order to get it running.

item.title: परछत्ती/Parchatti
item.description:
arjun - 28/07/2025, 11:55

what do you guys call this place ?

we call it parchatti

amanakeet - 01/08/2025, 08:14

Bukhari/Alyaa

item.title: CPT
item.description:
An entry from my journal, written in a midnight writers unblock initiated by Sarveshwar Dayal Saxena

कल पेपर है। और कल चंद मिनटों में आ जाएगा।

item.title: Unregulated
item.description: I like to support the untaxed, unregulated part of the economy. I don’t haggle with Rikshawalas. They aren’t technically unregulated, they are self regulating. They are perfect order within the lack of order. Nobody decided their prices, nobody made their laws. They operate a thin plane above supply and demand. Ethics and regulations when they exist are based on their morality. They have license plates, but who’s checking ?! Even when anarchy befalls, banks, governments and their issued tokens become obsolete I bet Rikshawalas would still be roaming on their screeching whining rikshaws, charged with stolen electricity. [1] [2]

item.title: चर्चा
item.description: बात होती है तो केवल उसकी जो स्पष्ट रूप से सभी की इंद्रियों में पंजिकृत [1] हो। तापमान, वायु का रुख, कुत्तों का भौंकना, विविद्ध प्रकार की गंधें वगैरा इस वार्तालाप के लिए उत्तम विष्य हैं।

item.title: Aloo Tikki
item.description: Its the conflicting flavours, red caramelized chutney with its heavy spicy taste and the green, sour tangy refreshing coriander chutney. The hot tikki just out of the wok, straight into some chilled yoghurt. A contrast of temperature. The depth and texture of the brown deep fried tikki being contrasted by the homogenous white yoghurt. On top it may be graced by a salad dressing, of chopped onions, cabbage and pomegranate further producing opposition to the deep fried carbohydrates of the tikki.

item.title: Music Reaction Channels
item.description: In the recent years, music reaction channels on YouTube have become quite a thing. These channels mainly react to forms of alternative music such as rock, metal and other genres that like to go with the “its not pop music” identity.

item.title: Make Youtube Distraction Free
item.description: Over the years, it has become increasingly hard to focus on the web. With websites and serveices now being designed to make you shuttle from one red dot to another, it has become necessary for us users to preserve our integrities as users and to not become consumers. This article will guide you to removing all sorts of distractions from YouTube, a popular video sharing website which houses a library of videos ranging from casual entertainment to perhaps the best lectures in calculus. For the ones who use this as a tool to study, it is not possible to use the platform while keeping together their focus on what’s important.

item.title: Short Video Format
item.description:
Against the short video format

The short video format is a non consensual, habit forming, freedom infringing video format that destroys the users attention span. It takes away the users right to consent, delivers them trivial information in bite sized bits of novelty along with a hit of hormones.

item.title: College
item.description:
A series of (incoherent) thoughts I have had since joining college.

The curriculum is merely a carcass every student is assigned to drag behind them for the next four years of their life. Even the administration knows this, and they don’t put too much effort into not letting the world know about it.
Exams and academics only exist to provide a goal to those who don’t have a purpose to live up to (yet).
Extra-curricular activities exist, yet as a curriculum themselves. The creative outlet of extra-curricular activities is just reduced to gaining credentials to add after ones name on their resume. Perhaps a form of totalitarianism, imposed by their political correctness. Giving us the freedom to have such collectives yet also prioritizing our careers and placements. Making it much harder to rebel against them. It is easy to rebel against tyranny but tough to rebel against the conformism enforced by the very freedom of choice. It is tough to realise when some granted freedoms of extra-curricular activities become the slaveries of imposed curriculum. This brings me to the question, shall we ever be truly free ? I tried to fit in, in fact if it has been noticed this webpage was reskinned (shall I say stripped of skin, or soul). For the purposes of looking more professional, to be more squared for professional life I had butchered my own creativity. Now that I have realised the status quo, I am no longer afraid of offending people by merely being not squared. This is some paranoia

item.title: Post Jee Feels
item.description: So my first JEE attempt is complete and now I am here, writing a blog post in a (sucessfull) attempt of relaxing and chilling for one day in what seems like months since I have walked without a head full of stress. This I belive must be my reward. The only proof that I need to strengthen this belife is that today, ladies and gentlemen is the day when the south western monsoon finally arrived here in Delhi.

item.title: Wearing A Mechanical Watch
item.description:
This article was made on my old blog on 17 February 2022.
Having worn almost all sorts of watches, around a month ago I finally decided to make the leap to a mechanical watch. This leap of mine which truly felt like a leap of faith in the beginning, was a lot more reliable than I had expected it to be.

item.title: Parantha Teir List
item.description: This post was originally published on my blogger blog on 6 February 2022. This is a revised version of that post.

item.title: The Only Day Your Dad Will Ever Ask You To Watch TV
item.description: This post was originally published on 27 January 2022 on my old blog. This is a revised version of the post.
